pattern: Per-index conversion costs turn this into the longest window whose sum stays within a budget, which a sliding window solves by extending the right edge and shrinking the left only when the budget is exceeded.
hints: Reduce the two strings to one array: the cost of matching each aligned character.
You now want the longest contiguous run whose total cost does not exceed the budget.
Slide a window: add the rightmost cost, and while the running cost exceeds maxCost advance the left edge; track the largest window width seen.

edge_inputs.description: uniform cost of one lets a length-three window fit
every position costs two so only one fits in budget three
zero budget allows only positions that already match
identical strings cost nothing so the whole string fits
expensive prefix forces the window into a cheaper suffix
budget large enough to cover the entire string

stress.description: random equal-length lowercase strings with a moderate budget
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    import string
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(n))
    t = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(n))
    cost = rng.randrange(0, 50)
    return [s, t, cost]
stress.note: random costs exercise frequent window shrinking
